description: Regression scenarios for deterministic Docker isolation setup, teardown, and residual-stack audit behavior.

evals.name: setup-writes-deterministic-port-map
expected_behavior: Derives a deterministic compose project name from the task name.
Allocates stable host ports for the requested variable names and writes them to the worktree env files.
Reports Status: DONE only after the scoped setup data is written.
anti_behavior: Choosing random ports without recording them.
Starting broad Docker cleanup outside the task scope.

evals.name: audit-reports-residual-stacks
expected_behavior: Checks for residual compose projects matching the ywc task naming convention.
Reports unexpected stacks as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED depending on collision risk.
Does not remove stacks unless --prune is explicitly supplied.
anti_behavior: Auditing every Docker project on the host as if it belonged to this repo.

evals.name: anti-flag-conflict
expected_behavior: Rejects the invocation before touching Docker or env files.
Returns NEEDS_CONTEXT with the conflicting mode flags.
anti_behavior: Picking setup because it appears first.
Running teardown after setup in one invocation.
Mutating files before resolving the mode conflict.
